General Info Visitor center with history exhibits and displays mostly on the second floor. A theater on main floor showing videos on demand and a second mini theater upstairs on the history of the Locks.Visitor center with history exhibits and displays mostly on the second floor. A theater on main floor showing videos on demand and a second mini theater upstairs on the history of the Locks.Other Links. http://ballardlocks.org/visitor-center-museum.html. ## Reviews. Be the first to review! It was a beautiful day and there were lots of people out enjoying the sun. There was a steady stream of different size boats going through the locks, which is surprisingly interesting to watch. I walked across the bridge to the official salmon ladder, but there was no activity there - those salmon were free styling behind the boats leaving the locks! Ballard Locks Visitor centre is good. We didn't get to see any boats come through, but it was still quite interesting to learn about the history and construction of the locks. It was a beautiful day, the grounds have a lot of different tree species and plants. Pros: free site; free parking; beautiful gardens; interesting (but lengthy) to watch locks fill or empty for boats to move through; salmon visible through underwater viewing windows Cons: salmon windows very dirty; not what the term “ladder” would suggest; too many other highlights in Seattle to compete with for time; need a car to access site.
